examples: PAKI AVE + PONI MOI RD -> Paki Ave + Poni Moi Rd
PAKI AVE + DIAMOND HEAD TENNIS CTR -> Paki Ave + Diamond Head Tennis Ctr
PAKI AVE + NOELA ST -> Paki Ave + Noela St
fixes.label: Recased shouting stop names

findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 3824 of 3824 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 36697 of 36697 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Rename stops to mixed case (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave').
findings.what: About 3807 stop names are written in ALL CAPS.
findings.why: Mixed-case names are easier to read in apps and are read more naturally by screen readers.

findings.effort: One field.
findings.fix: Add feed_contact_email to feed_info.txt.
findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.effort: Usually an export-tool fix, not hand editing.
findings.fix: Re-generate shape distances in your export; flag to your vendor if it persists.
findings.what: Some trips have stop times whose distances along the route go backwards.
findings.why: Apps can show buses jumping backwards or mis-order stops.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_timepoint_value' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing timepoint value (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
findings.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
findings.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already ended.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.effort: A few minutes in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Delete the empty calendars or set their service days.
findings.what: Some service calendars have no days of the week switched on.
findings.why: Trips tied to these calendars never run; they are dead data that can mask real schedule problems.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting.
findings.fix: Turn on 'remove unused shapes' (or the like) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

findings.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
findings.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
findings.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

findings.effort: Usually a re-export from your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Export and publish an updated GTFS feed that covers at least the next 30 days of service.
findings.what: This feed's service calendar runs out within the next 7 days.
findings.why: When the calendar ends, Google Maps and other trip planners drop your agency entirely. Riders see no service at all.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_with_shape_dist_traveled_but_no_shape_distances' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip with shape dist traveled but no shape distances (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unsorted_stop_times' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unsorted stop times (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_window_outside_feed_period' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Service window outside feed period (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Varies.
findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'big_gap_in_service' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Big gap in service (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 17 kinds of issue across 1562570 instances (2704 error, 1486383 warning, 73483 informational).

findings.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
findings.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
findings.what: Service data ended 947 day(s) ago.
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data ended 947 day(s) ago. Trip planners have likely already dropped this feed.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: This agency publishes realtime, but the feed needs an access key we don't have yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 1 validator error to resolve. Service data expired 945 days ago. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 0% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

fetch.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data expired 945 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

ntd_readiness.summary: Resolve this before you certify on the D-10. 1 validator error to resolve. Service data expired 945 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 3771 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("PAKI AVE + PONI MOI RD", "PAKI AVE + DIAMOND HEAD TENNIS CTR", "PAKI AVE + NOELA ST", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

findings.effort: A cleanup pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove stops no route serves, or add the trips that should call at them.
findings.what: 3 of 3824 boardable stops are never served by any trip.
findings.why: Riders see these stops in apps and on the map but can never catch anything there, which erodes trust in the data.

top_fixes.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
top_fixes.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

top_fixes.effort: Usually an export-tool fix, not hand editing.
top_fixes.fix: Re-generate shape distances in your export; flag to your vendor if it persists.
top_fixes.what: Some trips have stop times whose distances along the route go backwards.
top_fixes.why: Apps can show buses jumping backwards or mis-order stops.

top_fixes.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 3824 of 3824 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.
